finding: Onset: typically associated with chronic sarcoidosis

differentials.name: characteristic "apple jelly" color on diascopy
differentials.raw: characteristic "apple jelly" color on diascopy (= pressure applied with glass slide)

differentials.name: mild symptoms in spite of extensive radiographic changes (DIAGNOSTICALLY SIGNIFICANT):
differentials.raw: mild symptoms in spite of extensive radiographic changes (DIAGNOSTICALLY SIGNIFICANT):

differentials.name: fatigue, night sweats, weight loss, erythema nodosum
differentials.raw: fatigue, night sweats, weight loss, erythema nodosum

differentials.name: palpable peripheral lymphadenopathy
differentials.raw: palpable peripheral lymphadenopathy (⅓): discrete, nontender and movable @  Ophthalmic sarcoidosis (25-80%)

differentials.name: typically bilateral uveitis, photophobia, blurred vision, glaucoma
differentials.raw: typically bilateral uveitis, photophobia, blurred vision, glaucoma (rare)

context: Location:   face, posterior neck, previous trauma site Thoracic Sarcoidosis (90%) ◊  Extrathoracic manifestations without intrathoracic involvement in < 10%! Silzbach CXR stage versus percentage at presentation: 0   normal chest radiograph 5-10% 1   hilar + mediastinal lymphadenopathy only 50% 2   lymphadenopathy + parenchymal disease 25-30% 3   diffuse parenchymal disease only 10-12% 4   pulmonary fibrosis 5% ◊  Progression to pulmonary fibrosis in 25%!
